---
title: Difference between SVZ cells and UBC_1
date: 2024-07-24
metadata-files: 
  - _metadata.yml
  - ../_quarto.yml
execute:
  eval: false
---

# Goal of this analysis

1. Perform differential expression analysis betweem SVZ cells and UBC_1. Their expression patterns seem very similar. Want to understand the difference between the two.

# Files and directories

## Input

CCA RL file is found at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240618/rl_cca.qs`

SVZ cell IDs can be found at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240509/cca/svz_cells.csv`

Tumour file is found at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/icheong/CBL_scRNAseq/results/tumour/Vladoiu/20230510/mb_mnn.rds`

## Output

The output can be found at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240724`

# Process and Results

Using the `scrnaseq_env` conda environment

Lets load in the required libraries
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(Seurat)
library(qs)
library(jsonlite)
library(ggrepel)
library(patchwork)
```

```{r}
set.seed(1)
out_directory <- "/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240724"
if (!dir.exists(out_directory)) {
  dir.create(out_directory, recursive = TRUE)
}
```

Step 1: Load in datasets
```{r}
rl_cca <- qread("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240618/rl_cca.qs")
tumour_srat <- readRDS("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/icheong/CBL_scRNAseq/results/tumour/Vladoiu/20230510/mb_mnn.rds")
svz_cell_id <- read.csv("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240509/cca/svz_cells.csv")
```

Step 2: Split by original dataset
```{r}
ald <- subset(rl_cca, subset = dataset_name == "Aldinger_full_cerebellum_human")
sepp <- subset(rl_cca, subset = dataset_name == "Sepp_full_cerebellum_human")
```

Step 3: Take row sums for each gene in count matrix, keep genes expressed in 1% or more of cells
```{r}
# Get counts slot from Seurat object
counts_ald <- GetAssayData(object = ald, slot = "counts")
counts_sepp <- GetAssayData(object = sepp, slot = "counts")

# If a count for a gene in a cell is greater than 0, set as TRUE (= 1)
nonzero_ald <- counts_ald > 0
nonzero_sepp <- counts_sepp > 0

# If 1% or more cells are TRUE, keep the gene. Each TRUE value = 1. Taking the sum of all the cells for that gene
keep_ald_genes <- Matrix::rowSums(nonzero_ald) >= (0.01*length(Cells(ald)))
keep_sepp_genes <- Matrix::rowSums(nonzero_sepp) >= (0.01*length(Cells(sepp)))

# Get the genes names that we are keeping
ald_genes <- rownames(ald)[keep_ald_genes]
sepp_genes <- rownames(sepp)[keep_sepp_genes]
```

Step 4: Take intersecting genes between the datasets
```{r}
intersecting_genes <- intersect(ald_genes, sepp_genes)  
```

```{r}
# Note that the number of genes in the Aldinger and Sepp datasets are the same as they were merged together before
> print(length(rownames(ald)))
[1] 52672

> print(length(rownames(sepp)))
[1] 52672

> print(length(ald_genes))
[1] 9882

> print(length(sepp_genes))
[1] 12658

> print(length(intersecting_genes))
[1] 8122
```

Step 5: Filter each dataset for intersecting genes
```{r}
ald <- subset(ald, features = intersecting_genes)
sepp <- subset(sepp, features = intersecting_genes)
```

Step 6: Run SCTransform on each dataset, regressing out (CC.Difference)
```{r}
ald <- SCTransform(ald, vars.to.regress = "CC.Difference", variable.features.n = 5000)
sepp <- SCTransform(sepp, vars.to.regress = "CC.Difference", variable.features.n = 5000)
```

Step 7: Merge the datasets back together
```{r}
rl_merged <- merge(x = ald, y = sepp)
```

Step 8: Run PrepSCTFindMarkers()
```{r}
rl_merged <- PrepSCTFindMarkers(object = rl_merged)
```

Step 9: Create a new column in metadata indicating whether is a SVZ cell (Hendrikse labelled), UBC_1 from the Aldinger dataset, UBC_1 from the Sepp dataset, or other cell
```{r}
svz_id <- svz_cell_id$x

rl_merged$cell_id <- Cells(rl_merged)

rl_merged@meta.data <- rl_merged@meta.data %>% 
  mutate(
    # sexes from datasets
    svz_vs_ubc1 = case_when(
      cell_id %in% svz_id ~ "SVZ",
      new_clusters == "UBC_1" & dataset_name == "Aldinger_full_cerebellum_human" ~ "UBC_1_Ald",
      new_clusters == "UBC_1" & dataset_name == "Sepp_full_cerebellum_human" ~ "UBC_1_Sepp",
      TRUE ~ "other"
    )
  )

rl_merged$svz_vs_ubc1 <- factor(rl_merged$svz_vs_ubc1, levels = unique(rl_merged$svz_vs_ubc1))
```

Step 10: FindMarkers() with UBC_1 vs SVZ done separately for each dataset
```{r}
Idents(rl_merged) <- "svz_vs_ubc1"
ubc_1_markers_ald <- FindMarkers(rl_merged, ident.1 = "UBC_1_Ald", ident.2 = "SVZ") 
ubc_1_markers_sepp <- FindMarkers(rl_merged, ident.1 = "UBC_1_Sepp", ident.2 = "SVZ")

ubc_1_markers_ald %>%
    filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>%
    filter(pct.1 > 0.3 | pct.2 > 0.3) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs(avg_log2FC))) %>%
    slice_head(n = 50) %>%
    ungroup() -> top_features_ald

ubc_1_markers_ald$feature <- rownames(ubc_1_markers_ald)

ubc_1_markers_sepp %>%
    filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>%
    filter(pct.1 > 0.3 | pct.2 > 0.3) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs(avg_log2FC))) %>%
    slice_head(n = 50) %>%
    ungroup() -> top_features_sepp

ubc_1_markers_sepp$feature <- rownames(ubc_1_markers_sepp)
```

Load in TFs
```{r}
lambert_tf <- read.csv("/.mounts/labs/pailab/src/transcription-factors/human-tfs-lambert/full_database_v1.01.csv") %>%
    filter(Is.TF. == "Yes") %>%
    select(tf = HGNC.symbol) %>%
    unique()


hocomoco_tf <- readLines("/.mounts/labs/pailab/src/transcription-factors/HOCOMOCOv12/H12CORE_annotation.jsonl") %>% 
  lapply(fromJSON) %>%
  sapply(function(x) x$tf) %>%
  data.frame() %>%
  setNames("tf") %>%
  unique()
```

Take union of TF lists
```{r}
union_tf <- union(lambert_tf, hocomoco_tf)
```

Create a list of TF markers in genes for both datasets
```{r}
tf_markers_ald <- ubc_1_markers_ald %>%
  filter(rownames(.) %in% union_tf$tf) %>%
  arrange((avg_log2FC))

tf_markers_sepp <- ubc_1_markers_sepp %>%
  filter(rownames(.) %in% union_tf$tf) %>%
  arrange((avg_log2FC))
```

Below code wil generate a volcano plot for Aldinger all genes, Sepp all genes, Aldinger TFs, Sepp TFs and combine the 4 plots into one using patchwork.
```{r}
source("/u/llau/software/mb_scrnaseq/MB_scRNAseq/software/utilities/plotting.R")

variable_markers <- ubc_1_markers_ald

# Setting log2FC threshold
log_fc_thresh <- 0.5

# Adding column to determine if gene is up regulated or down regulated
variable_markers <- mutate(
  variable_markers,
  direction = case_when(
    variable_markers$avg_log2FC >= log_fc_thresh & variable_markers$p_val_adj < 0.05 ~ "Upregulated",
    variable_markers$avg_log2FC <= -log_fc_thresh & variable_markers$p_val_adj < 0.05 ~ "Downregulated",
    TRUE ~ "Not significant"
    )
  )
variable_markers$direction <- factor(variable_markers$direction, levels = c("Upregulated", "Downregulated", "Not significant"))

# Adding column denotating genes to label
variable_markers$feature_labelled <- ifelse(abs(variable_markers$avg_log2FC) > log_fc_thresh & variable_markers$p_val_adj < 0.05, variable_markers$feature, NA)

# Dealing with padjval = 0. -log10(0) = infinity therefore we need to deal with the 0 value by setting it as something small
non_zero <- variable_markers$p_val_adj[variable_markers$p_val_adj != 0]
# Find the smallest value
min_val <- min(non_zero)
# Multiply the smallest value by 0.1 and assign to p_val_adj values
variable_markers$p_val_adj <- ifelse(variable_markers$p_val_adj == 0, min_val*exp(-1), variable_markers$p_val_adj)

n_up <- sum(variable_markers$avg_log2FC > log_fc_thresh & variable_markers$p_val_adj < 0.05)
n_down <- sum(variable_markers$avg_log2FC < -log_fc_thresh & variable_markers$p_val_adj < 0.05)

gene_numbers <- paste0(
  "n = ", nrow(variable_markers),
  "; down = ", n_down,
  "; up = ", n_up
)

# Creating the volcano plot
plt_1 <- make_volcano(
  data = variable_markers,
  log_fc = avg_log2FC,
  log_pval = -log10(p_val_adj),
  direction = direction,
  log_fc_thresh = c(-log_fc_thresh, log_fc_thresh),
  log_pval_thresh = -log10(0.05),
  label_num_genes = TRUE
) + 
theme_classic() +
labs(x = expression(log[2]~"(fold change)"), y = expression(-log[10]~"(adj. p-value)")) +
scale_color_manual(values = c("Upregulated" = "red", "Downregulated" = "blue", "Not significant" = "grey")) +
ggtitle("UBC_1 vs SVZ: Aldinger Genes") +
theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) +
geom_text_repel(aes(label = feature_labelled), size = 4, show.legend = FALSE) +
labs(subtitle = gene_numbers)
```

```{r}
variable_markers <- ubc_1_markers_sepp

# Setting log2FC threshold
log_fc_thresh <- 0.5

# Adding column to determine if gene is up regulated or down regulated
variable_markers <- mutate(
  variable_markers,
  direction = case_when(
    variable_markers$avg_log2FC >= log_fc_thresh & variable_markers$p_val_adj < 0.05 ~ "Upregulated",
    variable_markers$avg_log2FC <= -log_fc_thresh & variable_markers$p_val_adj < 0.05 ~ "Downregulated",
    TRUE ~ "Not significant"
    )
  )
variable_markers$direction <- factor(variable_markers$direction, levels = c("Upregulated", "Downregulated", "Not significant"))

# Adding column denotating genes to label
variable_markers$feature_labelled <- ifelse(abs(variable_markers$avg_log2FC) > log_fc_thresh & variable_markers$p_val_adj < 0.05, variable_markers$feature, NA)

# Dealing with padjval = 0. -log10(0) = infinity therefore we need to deal with the 0 value by setting it as something small
non_zero <- variable_markers$p_val_adj[variable_markers$p_val_adj != 0]
# Find the smallest value
min_val <- min(non_zero)
# Multiply the smallest value by 0.1 and assign to p_val_adj values
variable_markers$p_val_adj <- ifelse(variable_markers$p_val_adj == 0, min_val*exp(-1), variable_markers$p_val_adj)

n_up <- sum(variable_markers$avg_log2FC > log_fc_thresh & variable_markers$p_val_adj < 0.05)
n_down <- sum(variable_markers$avg_log2FC < -log_fc_thresh & variable_markers$p_val_adj < 0.05)

gene_numbers <- paste0(
  "n = ", nrow(variable_markers),
  "; down = ", n_down,
  "; up = ", n_up
)

# Creating the volcano plot
plt_2 <- make_volcano(
  data = variable_markers,
  log_fc = avg_log2FC,
  log_pval = -log10(p_val_adj),
  direction = direction,
  log_fc_thresh = c(-log_fc_thresh, log_fc_thresh),
  log_pval_thresh = -log10(0.05),
  label_num_genes = TRUE
) + 
theme_classic() +
labs(x = expression(log[2]~"(fold change)"), y = expression(-log[10]~"(adj. p-value)")) +
scale_color_manual(values = c("Upregulated" = "red", "Downregulated" = "blue", "Not significant" = "grey")) +
ggtitle("UBC_1 vs SVZ: Sepp Genes") +
theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) +
geom_text_repel(aes(label = feature_labelled), size = 4, show.legend = FALSE) +
labs(subtitle = gene_numbers)
```
```{r}
variable_markers <- tf_markers_ald

# Setting log2FC threshold
log_fc_thresh <- 0

# Adding column denotating genes to label
variable_markers$feature_labelled <- ifelse(variable_markers$p_val_adj < 0.05, variable_markers$feature, NA)

# Adding column to determine if gene is up regulated or down regulated
variable_markers <- mutate(
  variable_markers,
  direction = case_when(
    variable_markers$avg_log2FC >= log_fc_thresh & variable_markers$p_val_adj < 0.05 ~ "Upregulated",
    variable_markers$avg_log2FC <= -log_fc_thresh & variable_markers$p_val_adj < 0.05 ~ "Downregulated",
    TRUE ~ "Not significant"
    )
  )
variable_markers$direction <- factor(variable_markers$direction, levels = c("Upregulated", "Downregulated", "Not significant"))

# Dealing with padjval = 0. -log10(0) = infinity therefore we need to deal with the 0 value by setting it as something small
non_zero <- variable_markers$p_val_adj[variable_markers$p_val_adj != 0]
# Find the smallest value
min_val <- min(non_zero)
# Multiply the smallest value by 0.1 and assign to p_val_adj values
variable_markers$p_val_adj <- ifelse(variable_markers$p_val_adj == 0, min_val*exp(-1), variable_markers$p_val_adj)

n_up <- sum(variable_markers$avg_log2FC > log_fc_thresh & variable_markers$p_val_adj < 0.05)
n_down <- sum(variable_markers$avg_log2FC < -log_fc_thresh & variable_markers$p_val_adj < 0.05)

gene_numbers <- paste0(
  "n = ", nrow(variable_markers),
  "; down = ", n_down,
  "; up = ", n_up
)

# Creating the volcano plot
plt_3 <- make_volcano(
  data = variable_markers,
  log_fc = avg_log2FC,
  log_pval = -log10(p_val_adj),
  log_fc_thresh = c(-log_fc_thresh, log_fc_thresh),
  log_pval_thresh = -log10(0.05),
  label_num_genes = TRUE
) + 
theme_classic() +
labs(x = expression(log[2]~"(fold change)"), y = expression(-log[10]~"(adj. p-value)")) +
geom_point(aes(color = direction)) +
scale_color_manual(values = c("Upregulated" = "red", "Downregulated" = "blue", "Not significant" = "grey")) +
ggtitle("UBC_1 vs SVZ: Aldinger TF") +
theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) +
geom_text_repel(aes(label = feature_labelled), size = 4, show.legend = FALSE) +
labs(subtitle = gene_numbers) +
theme(legend.position = "none")
```
```{r}
variable_markers <- tf_markers_sepp

# Setting log2FC threshold
log_fc_thresh <- 0

# Adding column denotating genes to label
variable_markers$feature_labelled <- ifelse(variable_markers$p_val_adj < 0.05, variable_markers$feature, NA)

# Adding column to determine if gene is up regulated or down regulated
variable_markers <- mutate(
  variable_markers,
  direction = case_when(
    variable_markers$avg_log2FC >= log_fc_thresh & variable_markers$p_val_adj < 0.05 ~ "Upregulated",
    variable_markers$avg_log2FC <= -log_fc_thresh & variable_markers$p_val_adj < 0.05 ~ "Downregulated",
    TRUE ~ "Not significant"
    )
  )
variable_markers$direction <- factor(variable_markers$direction, levels = c("Upregulated", "Downregulated", "Not significant"))

# Dealing with padjval = 0. -log10(0) = infinity therefore we need to deal with the 0 value by setting it as something small
non_zero <- variable_markers$p_val_adj[variable_markers$p_val_adj != 0]
# Find the smallest value
min_val <- min(non_zero)
# Multiply the smallest value by 0.1 and assign to p_val_adj values
variable_markers$p_val_adj <- ifelse(variable_markers$p_val_adj == 0, min_val*exp(-1), variable_markers$p_val_adj)

n_up <- sum(variable_markers$avg_log2FC > log_fc_thresh & variable_markers$p_val_adj < 0.05)
n_down <- sum(variable_markers$avg_log2FC < -log_fc_thresh & variable_markers$p_val_adj < 0.05)

gene_numbers <- paste0(
  "n = ", nrow(variable_markers),
  "; down = ", n_down,
  "; up = ", n_up
)

# Creating the volcano plot
plt_4 <- make_volcano(
  data = variable_markers,
  log_fc = avg_log2FC,
  log_pval = -log10(p_val_adj),
  log_fc_thresh = c(-log_fc_thresh, log_fc_thresh),
  log_pval_thresh = -log10(0.05),
  label_num_genes = TRUE
) + 
theme_classic() +
labs(x = expression(log[2]~"(fold change)"), y = expression(-log[10]~"(adj. p-value)")) +
geom_point(aes(color = direction)) +
scale_color_manual(values = c("Upregulated" = "red", "Downregulated" = "blue", "Not significant" = "grey")) +
ggtitle("UBC_1 vs SVZ: Sepp TF") +
theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) +
geom_text_repel(aes(label = feature_labelled), size = 4, show.legend = FALSE) +
labs(subtitle = gene_numbers) +
theme(legend.position = "none")
```

```{r}
volcano_plt <- wrap_plots(plt_1, plt_2, plt_3, plt_4, guides = "collect")
plot_name <- "SVZ_vs_UBC_1_volcano_combined.png"
ggsave(filename = plot_name, plot = volcano_plt , path = out_directory, 
          width = 10, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![Combined volcano plots for genes and TFs of Aldinger and Sepp dataset](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240724/SVZ_vs_UBC_1_volcano_combined.png)

Genes that seem interesting in differentiating the two clusters are:

TRPM3, ASIC2, NCKAP5, MKI67

Markers for SVZ from [the paper Figure 5](https://elifesciences.org/articles/81032) are: DPYD and SLC35F3

Interesting TF are RFX4, ZNF385B, E2F7, MECOM, OTX2, USH2A

```{r eval=FALSE}
# Retrieve UMAP coordinates
umap_coords <- Embeddings(rl_cca, "umap")

# Calculate minimum and maximum values for each dimension
min_umap <- apply(umap_coords, 2, min) # Min (x,y)
max_umap <- apply(umap_coords, 2, max) # Max (x,y)

min_x <- ceiling(min_umap[1])
min_y <- ceiling(min_umap[2])
max_x <- ceiling(max_umap[1])
max_y <- ceiling(max_umap[2])

buffer_x <- (max_x - min_x)*0.05
buffer_y <- (max_y - min_y)*0.05

min_x <- min_x - buffer_x
min_y <- min_y - buffer_y
max_x <- max_x + buffer_x
max_y <- max_y + buffer_y

DefaultAssay(rl_cca) <- "RNA"
DefaultAssay(tumour_srat) <- "RNA"
tumour_srat <- NormalizeData(tumour_srat)

genes = c("TRPM3", "ASIC2", "NCKAP5", "MKI67", "DPYD", "SLC35F3", "ZNF385B", "RFX4", "TP53", "E2F7", "MECOM")

for(gene in genes){
  feature_plot <- FeaturePlot(object = rl_cca, features = gene, order = TRUE, min.cutoff = "q10", max.cutoff = "q90", raster = FALSE) +
                  ggtitle(paste("Integrated: ", gene)) +
                  xlim(min_x, max_x) +       # Set x-axis limits
                  ylim(min_y, max_y)         # Set y-axis limits

  feature_plot_human <- FeaturePlot(object = rl_cca, cells = rownames(rl_cca@meta.data[rl_cca$species == "human", ]),  
                        features = gene, order = TRUE, min.cutoff = "q10", max.cutoff = "q90", raster = FALSE) +
                        ggtitle(paste("Human: ", gene)) +
                        xlim(min_x, max_x) +       # Set x-axis limits
                        ylim(min_y, max_y)         # Set y-axis limits

  feature_plot_mouse <- FeaturePlot(object = rl_cca, cells = rownames(rl_cca@meta.data[rl_cca$species == "mouse", ]), 
                        features = gene, order = TRUE, min.cutoff = "q10", max.cutoff = "q90", raster = FALSE) +
                        ggtitle(paste("Mouse: ", gene)) +
                        xlim(min_x, max_x) +       # Set x-axis limits
                        ylim(min_y, max_y)         # Set y-axis limits

  plot <- feature_plot | feature_plot_human | feature_plot_mouse
  feature_plot_name <- paste0(gene, ".png")
  ggsave(filename = feature_plot_name, plot = plot, path = out_directory, 
        width =30, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
  
  feature_plot <- FeaturePlot(object = tumour_srat, features = gene, order = TRUE, min.cutoff = "q10", max.cutoff = "q90", raster = FALSE) 

  feature_plot_name <- paste0(gene, "_tumour.png")
  ggsave(filename = feature_plot_name, plot = feature_plot, path = out_directory, 
        width =10, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
  
  }
```
